# Source Card: Evaluation of Proteinuria and Hematuria in Ambulatory Setting

## Summary
This 2022 peer-reviewed narrative review (Pediatric Clinics of North America) frames the outpatient/ambulatory pediatric evaluation of proteinuria and hematuria. According to PubMed, it establishes the qualitative classifications the CDS module needs — proteinuria as glomerular and/or tubular in origin and as transient, orthostatic, or persistent; hematuria as gross or microscopic and glomerular vs. urinary-tract in origin — and it names the key scope-exit signal: concurrent hematuria + proteinuria requires workup and monitoring, whereas isolated asymptomatic findings in a healthy child are less likely significant. Cite as [DOI](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.pcl.2022.07.002) (PMID 36880921). Numeric cutoffs stated in the (paywalled) body could not be locator-verified and are logged as GAPs below.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) Proteinuria is glomerular and/or tubular and may be transient, orthostatic, or persistent — locator: Abstract — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:b33d63999c24c36967c7c8c01ec9d614a69086b05d5fbd172592f895133c38f0]"
- (ev_002) Persistent proteinuria may indicate serious kidney pathology — locator: Abstract — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:6181af874f94ec1beaa9e47bb2fd4b3274f99699b18e488b9f4442952fec72a0]"
- (ev_003) Hematuria = increased urine RBCs; gross or microscopic — locator: Abstract — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:3b1f10b5f6c3e78132897971ac6139b3ab3de93f087993275d35d1bc2afcb11e]"
- (ev_004) Hematuria originates from glomeruli or elsewhere in the urinary tract — locator: Abstract — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:49e5c16c4cab9deb07c0dd588f9b28205331fdab0860cdc809ab6bb0e337ac89]"
- (ev_005) Isolated asymptomatic microscopic hematuria or mild proteinuria in a healthy child is less likely significant — locator: Abstract — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:044465ec4c274e34cb65422b19325532b9b658dc126ebc8c54fbe30b33ef14ee]"
- (ev_006) Concurrent hematuria + proteinuria requires workup and monitoring — locator: Abstract — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:8a63eb892ff230c5ee29bbf974a726592d0b036a90eb342194f56cb9ec741ac6]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population scope: children in the ambulatory/outpatient setting; specific age bands and sex-specific values are NOT stated in the accessible (abstract) text.
- Assay/method dependence: all extracted points are qualitative classifications; no analyzer-/method-dependent numeric thresholds could be locator-verified from the accessible text.
- Lifecycle: published 2022-10, Pediatr Clin North Am Vol. 69, Issue 6, pp. 1037-1049. No retirement/supersession recorded.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Yes — this source defines a referral/monitoring trigger: the co-occurrence of hematuria AND proteinuria "requires further workup and careful monitoring" (ev_006), while isolated asymptomatic findings in a healthy child are lower-concern (ev_005). This is a qualitative scope-exit signal, not a numeric rule; it is a PROPOSAL for the CDS converter, not an executable directive.

## Limitations & conflicts
- GAP (threshold not locatable in source): the microscopy definition of microscopic hematuria (RBCs per high-power field) is not present in the accessible abstract of Imam & Saadeh 2022; the numeric cutoff is expected in the paywalled body but could not be quote-verified — do NOT assert it from this card.
- GAP (threshold not locatable in source): the quantitative definitions of "mild" vs. nephrotic-range proteinuria and any urine protein:creatinine ratio cutoffs are not present in the accessible abstract; not asserted here.
- GAP (population not locatable): specific pediatric age bands and any sex-specific thresholds are not present in the accessible abstract.
- Access limitation: full text is paywalled (Elsevier ScienceDirect returned HTTP 403; no PubMed Central full-text copy available), so extraction is limited to the published abstract.
- Source tier: narrative review (secondary) — thresholds it cites originate from other primary/guideline sources; no conflicts recorded against other sources at this time.
